85	  %  is indeed a polynomial in \(m\) under the above assumption is a special
86	  %  case of what is known as Snapper's Lemma (see Kleiman [K] for a proof).
87	  %
88	  %  Let \(X\to S\) be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
89	  %  and let \(L\) be a line bundle on \(X\). Let \(\F\)
90	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
91	  %  \(X\) whose schematic support is proper over \(S\). Then for each \(s\in S\),
92	  %  we get a polynomial \({\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]\)
93	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of
94	  %  the restriction \(\F_s = F|_{X_s}\) of \(\F\) to the fiber \(X_s\) over \(s\),
95	  %  calculated with respect to the line bundle \(L_s = L|_{X_s}\).
96	  %  If \(\F\) is flat over \(S\) then the function \(s\mapsto {\Phi}_s\)
97	  %  from the set of points of \(S\) to the polynomial ring \(\Q[\lambda]\) is
98	  %  known to be locally constant on \(S\)."
99	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
100	  III.5.2.}
101	  Let \(X \to S\) be a finite-type morphism of noetherian schemes and let \(L\)
102	  be a line bundle on \(X\). For a coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) whose
103	  schematic support is proper over \(S\) and a point \(s \in S\), the
104	  \emph{Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}\) at \(s\) relative to \(L\)} is the
105	  polynomial \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\) characterised by
106	  \[
107	    \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)
108	    \;=\; \chi(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}|_{X_s} \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
109	    \;=\; \sum_{i \ge 0} (-1)^i \dim_{\kappa(s)} H^i(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}|_{X_s} \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
110	  \]
111	  (a polynomial in \(m\) by Snapper's Lemma; the sum is finite by coherence and
112	  properness). When \(\mathcal{F}\) is \(S\)-flat the function
113	  \(s \mapsto \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is locally constant on \(S\), so \(\Phi\) is
114	  well-defined on connected components.
115	\end{definition}
116	
117	\section{The Quot functor}
118	\label{sec:quot_functor}
119	
120	\begin{definition}\leanok
121	[Quot functor]
122	  \label{def:quot_functor}
123	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor}
124	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial}
125	
126	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "The Functors \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) and
127	  % \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\)" (read from
128	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L394-L433
129	  % + L481-L498).
130	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
131	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(X\to S\) be a finite type
132	  %  scheme over it. Let \(E\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X\).
133	  %  Let \(Sch_S\) denote the category of all locally noetherian schemes
134	  %  over \(S\). For any \(T\to S\) in \(Sch_S\), a \textbf{family of quotients of \(E\)
135	  %  parametrised by \(T\)} will mean
136	  %  a pair \((\F,q)\) consisting of
137	  %
138	  %  (i) a coherent sheaf \(\F\) on \(X_T = X\times_ST\)
139	  %  such that the schematic support of \(\F\) is proper over \(T\)
140	  %  and \(\F\) is flat over \(T\), together with
141	  %
142	  %  (ii) a surjective \({\mathcal O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism of sheaves
143	  %  \(q:E_T  \to \F\) where \(E_T\) is the pull-back of \(E\) under
144	  %  the projection \(X_T \to X\).
145	  %
146	  %  Two such families \((\F,q)\) and \((\F,q)\) parametrised by \(T\) will
147	  %  be regarded as equivalent if \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\)),
148	  %  and \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) will denote
149	  %  an equivalence class. Then as properness and flatness are preserved
150	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,
151	  %  the pull-back of \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) under an \(S\)-morphism \(T'\to T\) is
152	  %  well-defined, which gives
153	  %  a set-valued contravariant functor
154	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S} : Sch_S \to Sets\) under which
155	  %  \(\)T\mapsto  \{\mbox{ All } \langle \F, q \rangle \mbox{ parametrised by }T\}\(\)
156	  %  [...]
157	  %  This shows that the functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\) naturally decomposes
158	  %  as a co-product
159	  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S} = \coprod_{{\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]}\, \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\(\)
160	  %  where for any polynomial \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\), the functor
161	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) associates
162	  %  to any \(T\) the set of all equivalence classes of
163	  %  families \(\langle \F,q \rangle\) such that at each \(t\in T\) the
164	  %  Hilbert polynomial of the restriction \(\F_t\), calculated
165	  %  using the pull-back of \(L\), is \({\Phi}\)."
166	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
167	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
168	  \(L\) a line bundle on \(X\), \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\), and
169	  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). The \emph{Quot functor with Hilbert
170	  polynomial \(\Phi\)}
171	  \[
172	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}
173	  \]
174	  sends an \(S\)-scheme \(T \to S\) to the set of equivalence classes
175	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) where
176	  \begin{enumerate}
177	    \item \(\mathcal{F}\) is a coherent sheaf on \(X_T = X \times_S T\) whose
178	      schematic support is proper over \(T\) and which is flat over \(T\);
179	    \item \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is a surjective
180	      \(\mathcal{O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism, where \(E_T\) is the pull-back
181	      of \(E\) along \(X_T \to X\);
182	    \item for every \(t \in T\) the Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}\)
183	      with respect to \(L|_{X_t}\) (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
184	      \(\Phi\).
185	  \end{enumerate}
186	  Two pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) and \((\mathcal{F}', q')\) are equivalent iff
187	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). On morphisms \(f : T' \to T\) over \(S\), the functor
188	  acts by pulling back the quotient along \(X_{T'} \to X_T\) (well-defined
189	  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
190	  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
191	  \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\).
192	\end{definition}
193	
194	\section{The Grassmannian scheme}
195	\label{sec:grassmannian_scheme}
196	
197	The Quot construction proceeds by embedding \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) as a
198	locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian over \(S\). Since Mathlib carries
199	no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} (only a finite-rank-quotient variety in
200	linear algebra), we package the construction here.
201	
202	\begin{definition}\leanok
203	[Grassmannian scheme]
204	  \label{def:grassmannian_scheme}
205	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian}
206	  \uses{def:quot_functor}
207	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises~(2)" and
208	  % "Construction of Grassmannian" (read from
209	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L599
210	  % + L773-L850).
211	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
212	  % "For any integers \(r\ge d\ge 1\), an explicit construction
213	  %  the Grassmannian scheme
214	  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\), together with the tautological
215	  %  quotient \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) where \
216	  %  \(\U\) is a rank \(d\) locally free sheaf on \(\grass(r,d)\),
217	  %  has been given at the end of this section. [...]
218	  %  Show that \(\grass(r,d)\) together with the quotient
219	  %  \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) represents the
220	  %  contravariant functor
221	  %  \(\)\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}\(\)
222	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any \(T\) the
223	  %  set of all equivalence classes
224	  %  \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients \(q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F\)
225	  %  where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\).
226	  %  [...]
227	  %  Using the above show that, more generally, if \(S\) is a scheme and
228	  %  \(E\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\),
229	  %  the functor \(\Grass(E,d)=\Quot_{E/S/S}^{d,{\mathcal O}_S}\)
230	  %  on all \(S\)-schemes which
231	  %  by definition associates to any \(T\)
232	  %  the set of all equivalence classes \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients
233	  %  \(q: E_T \to \F\) where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\),
234	  %  is representable. The representing scheme
235	  %  is denoted by \(\grass(E,d)\) and is called the rank \(d\)
236	  %  relative Grassmannian of \(E\) over \(S\)."
237	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) and "Construction of
238	  Grassmannian" (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
239	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(V\) be a locally free
240	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\). For an integer \(1 \le d \le r\), the
241	  \emph{Grassmannian of rank-\(d\) quotients of \(V\)} is the functor
242	  \[
243	    \mathrm{Grass}(V, d) : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set},
244	    \qquad
245	    T \longmapsto \{ \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle :
246	    q : V_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F},\; \mathcal{F}
247	    \text{ locally free of rank } d \}
248	  \]
249	  with equivalence \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). Concretely \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d) =
250	  \Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}\) (the Quot functor for \(X = S\), \(E = V\),
251	  constant Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi = d\)).
252	
253	  Structurally \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d)\) is \emph{defined} as this Quot functor
254	  (\cref{def:quot_functor}) of the special case \(X = S\), \(E = V\),
255	  \(\Phi = d\) -- not as a second, independently-built functor. Fixing the
256	  definition this way makes the identity
257	  \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d) = \Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}\) hold by
258	  construction, so the structural correspondence is preserved automatically
259	  when the underlying \(\Quot\) construction is later filled in, and the
260	  Grassmannian inherits its functoriality and equivalence relation from the
261	  Quot functor rather than restating them.
262	\end{definition}
263	
264	\begin{lemma}[Representability predicate]
265	  \label{lem:functor_is_representable_mathlib}
266	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.IsRepresentable}
267	  \mathlibok
268	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
269	  Let \(\mathcal{C}\) be a category and \(F : \mathcal{C}^{op} \to \mathbf{Set}\)
270	  a presheaf of sets. The predicate \(F.\mathrm{IsRepresentable}\) asserts that
271	  \(F\) is representable. It unfolds -- via
272	  \texttt{Functor.IsRepresentable.has\_representation} -- to exactly
273	  \[
274	    \exists\,Y,\;\; \mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl(F.\mathrm{RepresentableBy}\,Y\bigr),
275	  \]
276	  i.e.\ the existence of an object \(Y\) of \(\mathcal{C}\) together with a
277	  natural bijection \(\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(-, Y) \cong F\). When it holds,
278	  \texttt{Functor.representableBy} produces the canonical witness
279	  \(F.\mathrm{RepresentableBy}\,(F.\mathrm{reprX})\) on the representing object
280	  \(F.\mathrm{reprX}\).
281	\end{lemma}
282	
283	\begin{theorem}\leanok
284	[Representability of the Grassmannian]
285	  \label{thm:grassmannian_representable}
286	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable}
287	  \uses{def:grassmannian_scheme, thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_univ,
288	    lem:functor_is_representable_mathlib}
289	
290	  % NOTE (representability proof blocked): the sketch below recovers the
291	  % representing object through the RepresentableBy Yoneda form "as in the
292	  % RelativeSpec chapter" (\cref{thm:relative_spec_univ}). But
293	  % thm:relative_spec_univ is currently established at the Lean level only as
294	  % "IsAffineHom (structureMorphism A)", which is strictly weaker than a
295	  % CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy witness. The PROOF of
296	  % thm:grassmannian_representable is therefore blocked on either (a)
297	  % strengthening thm:relative_spec_univ to deliver a RepresentableBy witness,
298	  % or (b) finding a RepresentableBy-free Grassmannian argument (gluing the
299	  % chart-wise classifying morphisms into the representing object directly).
300	  % This is a deferred open question, not resolved in the present pass; the
301	  % RelativeSpec chapter is not edited here.
302	
303	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
304	  % affine patches" through "Projective embedding"
305	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
306	  % L798-L930).
307	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
308	  % "For any integers \(r\ge d\ge 1\), the Grassmannian scheme
309	  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\), together with the tautological
310	  %  quotient \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) where \
311	  %  \(\U\) is a rank \(d\) locally free sheaf on \(\grass(r,d)\),
312	  %  can be explicitly constructed as follows. [...]
313	  %  the schemes
314	  %  \(U^I\), as \(I\) varies over all the
315	  %  \({r\choose d}\) different
316	  %  subsets of  \(\{ 1,\ldots, r\}\) of cardinality \(d\),
317	  %  can be glued together by the co-cycle \((\theta_{I,J})\) to form a
318	  %  finite-type scheme \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\). As each \(U^I\) is
319	  %  isomorphic to \(\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}\), it follows that
320	  %  \(\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z\) is smooth of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\).
321	  %  [...]
322	  %  We now show that \(\pi : \grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z\) is proper. It is enough to
323	  %  verify the valuative criterion of properness for discrete valuation rings.
324	  %  [...]
325	  %  As \(\U\) is given by the transition functions \(g_{I,J}\) described above,
326	  %  the determinant line bundle
327	  %  \(\det(\U)\) is given by the transition functions
328	  %  \(\det(g_{I,J}) = 1/P^I_J \in GL_1(U^I_J)\).
329	  %  [...]
330	  %  We leave it to the reader to verify that the sections \(\sigma_I\) form
331	  %  a linear system which is base point free and separates points
332	  %  relative to \(\spec \Z\), and so gives an embedding of
333	  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) into \(\PP^m_{\Z}\) where \(m = {r\choose d}-1\).
334	  %  This is a closed embedding by the properness of
335	  %  \(\pi:\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z\). In particular, \(\det(\U)\)
336	  %  is a relatively very ample line bundle on \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\)."
337	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Construction of Grassmannian" (FGA
338	  Explained Ch.~5).}
339	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(V\) a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module
340	  of rank \(r\), and \(1 \le d \le r\). The functor \(\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\) of
341	  \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} is representable by a smooth projective
342	  \(S\)-scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \to S\) of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\),
343	  equipped with a tautological quotient
344	  \(\pi^* V \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) of rank \(d\). The determinant
345	  line bundle \(\det(\mathcal{U})\) is relatively very ample, and the
346	  associated linear system gives a closed embedding
347	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \hookrightarrow
348	  \mathbb{P}_S\!\left(\bigwedge^d V\right)\) -- the \emph{Pl\"ucker embedding}.
349	
350	  \emph{Faithful target.} The representability conclusion is exactly Mathlib's
351	  representability predicate (\cref{lem:functor_is_representable_mathlib}): the
352	  intended statement is \((\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)).\mathrm{IsRepresentable}\), whose
353	  unfolding \texttt{Functor.IsRepresentable.has\_representation} is precisely
354	  \(\exists\,Y : \mathrm{Over}\,S,\ \mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl((\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)).\mathrm{RepresentableBy}\,Y\bigr)\).
355	  Stating the theorem in the \texttt{IsRepresentable} spelling lets the parent
356	  project recover the representing \(S\)-scheme and the Yoneda bijection
357	  directly through \texttt{Functor.representableBy} / \texttt{Functor.reprX} at
358	  merge-back, instead of re-bridging an ad-hoc existential.
359	
360	  \emph{Universe constraint.} \(\mathrm{RepresentableBy}\,F\,Y\) is available
361	  only for \(F : \mathcal{C}^{op} \to \mathrm{Type}\,v\) whose representing
362	  object \(Y\) lies in the \emph{same} category \(\mathcal{C}\) at the
363	  \emph{matching} hom-universe \(v\). Hence the target universe of the
364	  Grassmannian functor (and of the Quot functor of \cref{def:quot_functor} on
365	  which it is built) must be pinned to the hom-universe of the schemes category
366	  \(\mathrm{Sch}/S\) -- equivalently of \(\mathrm{Over}\,S\), in which the
367	  representing object \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V,d)\) lives. If the functor's value
368	  universe is left free, the representability statement does not typecheck.
369	\end{theorem}
370	
371	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the "Construction of Grassmannian" sub-section of
372	% [Nitsure], \S 1 (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
373	% L773-L930), which gives the construction in five named steps
374	% ("Construction by gluing together affine patches", "Separatedness",
375	% "Properness", "Universal quotient", "Projective embedding"). The total
376	% verbatim text is ~150 lines; it is reproduced piecewise in the
377	% SOURCE QUOTE comment of the theorem statement above.
378	\begin{proof}
379	  
380	  Over \(S = \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) and \(V = \mathcal{O}_S^{\oplus r}\) the
381	  Grassmannian \(\mathrm{Gr}(r, d)\) is constructed by gluing
382	  \(\binom{r}{d}\) affine charts \(U^I = \Spec\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) indexed by
383	  size-\(d\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\). The chart \(U^I\)
384	  carries the matrix \(X^I\) of size \(d \times r\) whose \(I\)-th \(d \times d\)
385	  minor is the identity, with the remaining \(d(r-d)\) entries free
386	  variables (so \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\)). The
387	  transition maps \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I \cap U^J \to U^J \cap U^I\) defined
388	  by \(X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) on the locus where \(\det X^I_J\) is
389	  invertible satisfy the cocycle condition
390	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\). The glued
391	  \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec\mathbb{Z}\) is smooth of relative dimension
392	  \(d(r-d)\). Separatedness is checked from the diagonal
393	  \(\Delta_{I,J} \subset U^I \times U^J\) cut out by \(X^J_I X^I - X^J = 0\).
394	  Properness follows from the valuative criterion for DVRs: given
395	  \(\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over a DVR \(R\) with fraction
396	  field \(K\), factoring through some chart \(U^I\) via a ring map
397	  \(f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K\), choose \(J\) minimising the valuation
398	  \(\nu(f(P^I_J))\) of the minor; on the chart \(U^J\) all entries have
399	  non-negative valuation, so \(f\) extends uniquely to \(\Spec R\).
400	
401	  The universal quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\oplus r}
402	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) is given on \(U^I\) by the matrix \(X^I\)
403	  and glued by \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I \cap U^J)\). The
404	  determinant line bundle \(\det\mathcal{U}\) has transition functions
405	  \(\det g_{I,J} = 1/P^I_J\); the global sections
406	  \(\sigma_I \in \Gamma(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), \det\mathcal{U})\) defined by
407	  \(\sigma_I|_{U^J} = P^J_I\) form a base-point-free linear system separating
408	  points over \(\Spec\mathbb{Z}\), giving an embedding into
409	  \(\mathbb{P}^{\binom{r}{d}-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}\), which is closed by
410	  properness. Representability of \(\mathrm{Grass}(r,d)\) is then verified
411	  on the universal pair \((\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), u)\): a quotient
412	  \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\oplus r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) of
413	  rank \(d\) on \(T\) is everywhere locally trivialised; on the locus where
414	  the columns indexed by \(I\) are a basis the matrix of \(q\) has the form
415	  \(X^I\), defining the classifying morphism \(T \to U^I \subset
416	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Base change from \(\mathbb{Z}\) to a general noetherian
417	  base \(S\) with a vector bundle \(V\) is direct: trivialise \(V\) on an open
418	  cover and glue. For non-trivial \(V\) the same construction produces
419	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) with Pl\"ucker embedding into
420	  \(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\).
421	\end{proof}
422	
423	\section{Lean encoding}
424	\label{sec:quot_lean_encoding}
425	
426	The Lean target file is
427	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. The Quot-foundations
428	layer proceeds in two phases.
429	
430	\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
431	On a finite-type morphism \(\pi : X \to S\) with \(S\) noetherian and a
432	coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) with proper support over \(S\), the per-fiber
433	Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a function \(s \mapsto
434	\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). Mathlib provides
435	Euler-characteristic infrastructure on coherent sheaves on noetherian
436	schemes; the polynomial property of
437	\(m \mapsto \chi(\mathcal{F} \otimes L^{\otimes m})\) requires
438	Snapper's Lemma, which is a project-side sub-build on top of the
439	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure.
440	
441	\emph{Intended signature of \(\mathrm{hilbertPolynomial}\).} Mathlib at the pin
442	carries no single \texttt{IsCoherent} predicate; coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\)
443	over the locally noetherian base is the conjunction of the two predicates that
444	do exist, \texttt{SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent} and
445	\texttt{SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType} -- i.e.\ the hypotheses
446	\([\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}]\) and
447	\([\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsFiniteType}]\) (and likewise for the line bundle
448	\(L\)). The properness-of-support hypothesis
449	``\(\operatorname{supp}\mathcal{F}\) proper over \(S\)'' has \emph{no} clean
450	Mathlib predicate. The minimal honest encoding forms the schematic support of
451	\(\mathcal{F}\) as a closed subscheme \(Z \hookrightarrow X\) and requires the
452	composite \(Z \hookrightarrow X \xrightarrow{\pi} S\) to satisfy
453	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper}; since the schematic-support closed
454	subscheme of a coherent sheaf is not itself available at the pin, this support
455	datum is recorded as a project-side gap to be supplied alongside the
456	definition. (The existing Lean pin
457	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial} is retained; adding these
458	hypotheses to its signature is a later refactor of the \texttt{.lean} stub, not
459	done in this pass.)
460	
461	\paragraph{Phase 2: Quot and Grassmannian functors
462	(\cref{def:quot_functor}, \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme},
463	\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).} The Quot functor
464	\(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) is encoded as a contravariant functor on
465	\((\mathrm{Sch}/S)\) of \(T\)-flat coherent quotients; the Grassmannian
466	\(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d)\) is the special case \(X = S\), \(E = V\), constant
467	Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi = d\). The representing scheme
468	\(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) is the relative gluing of \(\binom{r}{d}\)
469	affine charts \(U^I\) (each isomorphic to a relative affine space
470	\(\mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_S\)) via the Pl\"ucker cocycle \(\theta_{I,J}\). The
471	gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData}. The
472	universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) is glued by the transition cocycle
473	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the determinant line bundle
474	\(\det \mathcal{U}\) gives the Pl\"ucker closed embedding into
475	\(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\). The representability statement is pinned to
476	Mathlib's \texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.IsRepresentable}
477	(\cref{lem:functor_is_representable_mathlib}), which packages exactly the
478	\texttt{RepresentableBy} witness on the universal pair
479	\((\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d), \mathcal{U})\), in the same Yoneda style as
480	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}.
481	
482	\emph{Intended signatures.} Coherence of \(E\) in \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) is
483	encoded as in Phase 1, by the conjunction \([E.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}]\) and
484	\([E.\mathrm{IsFiniteType}]\) over the locally noetherian base (no single
485	\texttt{IsCoherent} predicate exists at the pin). For \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d)\),
486	the hypothesis ``\(V\) locally free of rank \(r\)'' has no faithful Mathlib
487	predicate at the pin: \texttt{SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFree} appears only
488	upstream of the pinned commit and is in any case rank-agnostic (it fixes no
489	value \(r\)). The intended encoding therefore pairs an
490	\texttt{IsLocallyFree}-style hypothesis on \(V\) with an explicit rank
491	\(r : \mathbb{N}\) and the constraint \(1 \le d \le r\) carried as a hypothesis
492	on naturals; the rank-\(r\) local-freeness predicate is flagged as a
493	project-side gap to be supplied. Consistently with
494	\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}, \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d)\) is \emph{defined} as
495	\(\Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}\) (the case \(X = S\), \(E = V\),
496	\(\Phi = d\)) rather than as an independent functor. The existing Lean pins on
497	\(\Quot\) and \(\mathrm{Grass}\) are retained; threading these hypotheses
498	through the \texttt{.lean} signatures is a later refactor.
499	
500	\section{Out of scope}
501	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
502	
503	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-foundations layer: the Hilbert
504	polynomial, the Quot functor, and the Grassmannian scheme with its
505	representability. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
506	\begin{itemize}
507	  \item \textbf{Representability of the Quot scheme} -- the
508	    Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman existence theorem and its proof skeleton
509	    (boundedness, Grassmannian embedding, flattening stratification,
510	    valuative criterion) are not constructed in this chapter.
511	  \item \textbf{Flat base change of cohomology} (Stacks 02KH) and its
512	    project-side substrate -- not constructed in this chapter.
513	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
514	    \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) is the special case
515	    \(\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\) of the Quot functor and is
516	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
517	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
518	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
519	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;
520	    project-side sub-build for the Lean encoding.
521	  \item \textbf{Identification with \(\mathbb{P}^n\) and relative
522	    Grassmannian variants} (Nitsure \S 1, Exercises (1)--(4)) --
523	    optional applications, not consumed by Route A.
524	  \item \textbf{Group-scheme structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)} (A.2.c) -- the
525	    abelian-group-scheme refinement is downstream. Its blueprint home is the
526	    FGA Picard-representability assembly (the chapter that bundles the relative
527	    Picard functor, its representability, and the abelian-group-scheme
528	    structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)); that assembly lives in the parent project
529	    which consumes this Quot-foundations layer and is not part of the present
530	    subproject.
531	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety
532	    structure} (A.3) -- a separate Route A sub-row, fed by but not
533	    constructed in this chapter.
534	\end{itemize}

*(GR-cells + GR-glue decomposition of QUOT-repr)*

**Key declarations** (in dependency order):
1. `\definition` `\label{def:gr_affine_chart}` — for each size-d subset I ⊆ {1,...,r}, the affine chart U^I := Spec(ℤ[X^I]) where X^I is the d×r matrix with identity d×d I-block and d(r-d) free entries; U^I ≅ 𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart}` [expected]. Source: Nitsure §1 "Construction of Grassmannian" (`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex`, L773–L810).
2. `\definition` `\label{def:gr_transition}` — for I ≠ J, the transition map θ_{I,J} : U^I_J → U^J_I defined by X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ · X^I on the locus where det(X^I_J) ≠ 0. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionMap}` [expected]. Source: Nitsure §1, L810–L820.
3. `\lemma` `\label{lem:gr_cocycle}` — the transition maps satisfy the cocycle condition θ_{I,K} = θ_{J,K} ∘ θ_{I,J} on U^I ∩ U^J ∩ U^K. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.cocycleCondition}` [expected]. Source: Nitsure §1; follows from associativity of matrix multiplication. The key Lean infrastructure is `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData.cocycle_cond`.
4. `\definition` `\label{def:gr_glued_scheme}` — the Grassmannian scheme Gr(r,d) over ℤ obtained by gluing {U^I}_{|I|=d} along {θ_{I,J}} using `Scheme.GlueData`. Smooth over ℤ of relative dimension d(r-d). `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme}` [expected]. Source: Nitsure §1, L820–L830.
5. `\lemma` `\label{lem:gr_separated}` — Gr(r,d) is separated over ℤ: the diagonal Δ ↪ Gr(r,d) ×_ℤ Gr(r,d) is a closed immersion, checked on charts by X^J_I · X^I = X^J. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isSeparated}` [expected]. Source: Nitsure §1, L830–L840.
6. `\lemma` `\label{lem:gr_proper}` — Gr(r,d) is proper over ℤ: valuative criterion for DVRs, extending over the DVR by choosing the chart with minimal-valuation minor. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isProper}` [expected]. Source: Nitsure §1, L840–L860.

- `lem:gr_proper` uses `def:gr_glued_scheme`, `lem:gr_separated`
- `lem:gr_cocycle` uses `def:gr_transition`, `def:gr_affine_chart`

**Main theorem proof strategy**: The cells U^I are affine spaces; the cocycle condition is a matrix-algebra identity; the gluing uses `Scheme.GlueData`. Separatedness reduces to a diagonal equation on charts. Properness uses the valuative criterion: a map from Spec(K) to Gr(r,d) extends to Spec(R) by choosing the chart with minimal-valuation minor. Each step is elementary matrix algebra over a DVR.
